questions.question: Which of the following was NOT a feature of the Green Revolution in India?
questions.option_2: Increased use of fertilisers and irrigation

questions.question: What three-part economic strategy defined India’s policy changes in the 1990s?
Why is infrastructure referred to as the 'wheels' of economic growth?
questions.option_1: It enables delivery of goods, services and access.
questions.option_2: It is part of international trade.

questions.question: Human development emphasises enlarging people’s choices by improving health, education, and income. Which global organisation publishes the Human Development Index (HDI)?
What was the major aim of India’s 1991 economic reforms?
questions.option_4: To reduce taxes on luxury goods

questions.question: Tertiary activities are also known as the:
Which of the following factors has least contributed to rural distress in India?
